codeReview.summary: Excellent implementation that fully solves the content-less email digest problem through defensive normalization, comprehensive fallbacks, and dependency injection for testability. All required behaviors verified. Code quality is high with proper TDD approach, clear separation of concerns, and no regressions introduced.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker demonstrated strong adherence to existing codebase patterns — used existing mutex patterns for thread safety, followed Go idioms for error handling and normalization, and maintained consistent naming conventions. No convention gaps identified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Code review shows consistent use of strings.TrimSpace(), proper mutex Lock/Unlock patterns matching existing DigestCollector code, and idiomatic Go switch statements in normalizeDigestEvent() and eventBadgeStyle().

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed go-worker skill procedure correctly: read digest.go and all Collect() sites (understanding phase), wrote digest_test.go first (TDD red phase), implemented changes systematically (green phase), verified with all validators, and committed with proper format 'cogent(notify): fix digest email content'. handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true is accurate.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows Read tools for digest.go and service.go Collect() sites before any edits, test file created and run before final implementation (TDD), all verification commands from go-worker skill executed (go build, go test filtered, go vet), commit message follows cogent(<scope>): format.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker efficiently located all relevant code paths (3 Collect() call sites in service.go, hourly flush in serve.go, SendEmail usage) using grep/ripgrep. No evidence of time wasted searching for information that should be documented. The email digest architecture was straightforward enough to understand from code inspection.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows targeted grep calls: '.Collect(' in service.go, 'SendEmail(' across codebase, 'FlushDigest' in serve.go. Worker found all relevant sites quickly without excessive exploration.

summary: PASS - High-quality implementation that comprehensively fixes content-less email digests. Root cause: DigestItem data was incomplete and rendering had no fallbacks. Fix: (1) Added Objective field to DigestItem for richer context, (2) Implemented normalizeDigestItem() to fill missing Title/Summary with fallbacks (Objective → defaults), (3) Built meaningful subjects via buildDigestSubject() with event counts and work titles, (4) Refactored all Collect() sites to use digestItemForWork() helper ensuring complete data, (5) Added dependency injection (digestSender) enabling proper unit testing. New test TestDigestCollectorFlushBuildsMeaningfulEmail verifies subject/body contain real work titles, IDs, objectives, and summaries. All validators pass (go build, filtered go test, go vet). Verified: (a) only SendSpecEscalationEmail calls SendEmail directly, (b) hourly digestTicker in serve.go calls FlushDigest. No blocking or non-blocking issues. Worker followed skill procedure perfectly. Fulfills VAL-EMAIL-001 through VAL-EMAIL-004. Production-ready.
